Risk: Informal agents promise AFCON access for money
Warning Sign: Unverified person asks for payment or guarantees role
Mitigation: Use only official channels and trusted referrals; never pay for vague opportunities
Notes: Verify with CAF/FUFA/government/credible institution before action.

Risk: Positioning undersells Rachael as a beginner
Warning Sign: Messages ask for any work or internship only
Mitigation: Use senior creative consultant framing while allowing learning/shadowing as secondary route

Warning Sign: Recipients do not understand what she offers
Mitigation: Use specific offer: creative direction choreography cultural programming visitor experience diaspora tourism
Notes: Every message needs a clear ask.

Warning Sign: Tracker has institutions but no named departments or people
Mitigation: Research and add verified contact persons gradually
Notes: Do not invent emails or phone numbers.

Mitigation: Parallel private-sector outreach to hotels agencies venues and sponsors

Warning Sign: Role demands major labour without access learning credit or portfolio
Mitigation: Accept unpaid only if it creates references evidence learning or strategic access

Risk: Local artists feel bypassed by diaspora profile
Mitigation: Frame Rachael as bridge mentor and collaborator not replacement

Risk: Concept appears too ambitious for first stage
Warning Sign: Partners ask for budget or implementation proof
Mitigation: Offer small cafe-arts pilot first then scale

Warning Sign: Official timelines shift or opportunities delayed
Mitigation: Keep plan flexible and focus on tourism/culture legacy and pilots not only official tournament roles
Notes: Useful even if official timelines change.

Mitigation: Use only approved materials and provide website/credits where permitted

Warning Sign: Messages sent but tracker not updated
Mitigation: Use weekly review and follow-up dates in tracker

Warning Sign: Partners ask about costs and team cannot answer
Mitigation: Prepare mini budget with low/medium/high options
